name: ASSR follow-up recommendation pattern
typicalFindings: Findings may be consistent with the pattern when history, audiologic results, and cross-check measures align. Audiologic findings alone are not diagnostic.
symptoms: Symptoms vary and may include hearing difficulty, tinnitus, dizziness, imbalance, sound sensitivity, device difficulty, or listening fatigue.
tympanometry: May be normal or abnormal depending on outer-ear and middle-ear involvement. Interpret with otoscopy and history.
reflexes: May be present, elevated, absent, decayed, or inconsistent depending on the pattern. Use cautiously with other measures.
oaes: May be present, absent, reduced, or inconsistent depending on cochlear outer hair cell status and middle-ear transmission.
speech: Speech testing may confirm functional listening difficulty, reveal disproportionate word recognition, or show speech-in-noise deficits not predicted by the audiogram.
vestibular: Relevant when dizziness, vertigo, imbalance, falls, sound-induced dizziness, pressure-induced dizziness, or hearing-balance patterns are reported.
nextBattery: Consider otoscopy, immittance, pure-tone thresholds, speech testing, speech-in-noise, OAEs, acoustic reflexes, ABR, vestibular testing, aided testing, or medical referral depending on the pattern.
referral: Consider ENT, otology, neurology, genetics, vestibular PT, CI center, pediatrician, or urgent care based on red flags and scope.
counseling: Use cautious language: findings may be consistent with this pattern, additional testing may be needed, and medical diagnosis requires the appropriate provider.
notConfirm: This pattern does not confirm a medical diagnosis by itself.
